conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, let me read the official guidance to ensure I provide you with accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex, who is 4 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 4 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's helpful to know.

**Do you live with Alex, or does he live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Just one more question to complete my assessment:

**Are you responsible for Alex's care - meaning you provide for his needs such as food, clothes, and general upkeep?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I look after all his needs - his food, clothes, everything he needs day to day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarize your situation:
- You have one child, Alex, who is 4 years old
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex lives with you at home
- You are responsible for his care and provide for all his needs (food, clothes, etc.)
- Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex

Based on the official government guidance, you meet all the key eligibility criteria for Child Benefit:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You live in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex (you live with him and provide for his needs)
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and nobody else is claiming for Alex

You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex!

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
You should be able to apply for Child Benefit for Alex. If you haven't already applied, you can do so through the official government website or by contacting HMRC.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 4 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 4 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 4 years old (under 16), so meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. The claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for his care, providing for all his needs including food and clothes, which meets the responsibility criteria. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, satisfying the requirement that only one person can claim for a child. All eligibility criteria under the Child Benefit rules are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 4), lives in the UK, lives with and cares for Alex, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All standard eligibility criteria are satisfied.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 4 years old (under 16), so meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. The claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for his care, providing for all his needs including food and clothes, which meets the responsibility criteria. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, satisfying the requirement that only one person can claim for a child. All eligibility criteria under the Child Benefit rules are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 4), lives in the UK, lives with and cares for Alex, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All standard eligibility criteria are satisfied.
